metadata.author: Obadiah
metadata.subject: The Judgment of Edom for Their Cruelty Toward Israel and the Final Victory of the Kingdom of Jehovah.

1: The vision of Obadiah. Thus says the Lord Jehovah concerning Edom: We have heard a report from Jehovah, and an envoy has been sent among the nations: Arise, and let us rise up against her for battle!
Though you soar like the eagle, and though your nest is set among the stars, from there I will bring you down, declares Jehovah.
For the violence done to your brother Jacob, shame will cover you, and you will be cut off forever.
But on Mount Zion there will be those who escape, and it will be holy; and the house of Jacob will possess their possessions.
And the kingdom will be Jehovah's.

3.text: Edom represents the natural man, the descendants of Esau, who are often in conflict with the spiritual man, represented by Jacob.

10.text: The sin of Edom was particularly grievous because they rejoiced in the calamity of their own kin, the people of Israel.

21.text: The book concludes with the ultimate sovereignty of God, where all earthly powers and natural oppositions are subdued under His reign.
